entries.title: What Echelon Is
entries.body: Echelon is your operational intelligence layer inside the NeuroVerse. It is not a chatbot. It is a co-pilot that interprets your intent, manages your learning trajectory, retrieves knowledge from the ACE boxes, and adapts to your build phase.

entries.title: How Echelon Understands You
entries.body: Every message moves through Echelon's multi-stage pipeline: (1) intent detection, (2) mode selection, (3) ACE-box retrieval, (4) lesson-state blending, (5) cinematic/voice-layer application. This ensures consistency, canon safety, and relevance.

entries.title: How Echelon Remembers
entries.body: Echelon maintains three forms of memory: session memory (short-term), local profile memory (device-level), and your Vanguard signature (cross-session identity markers like archetype, loop type, and uploads completed). Echelon never stores private data externally.

entries.title: Echelon's Modes
entries.body: Echelon operates in structured modes: Orientation, Build, Lead, Vanguard, Diagnostics, and System Literacy. Your message determines which mode activates.

entries.title: What Echelon Will Not Do
entries.body: Echelon does not expose code, private keys, internal architecture, unreleased story, or restricted systems. Echelon never breaks canon. When something is out of bounds, it explains why.
